groups.title: Free/low-entry subs and a cheap first PPV to build the spending habit
Price per fan by traffic source, proven spend, and purchase history
Sell via an escalating PPV price ladder, not one flat ask
Flex deal structure to creator tier instead of a one-size split
Build rapport and let the fan initiate before selling; never open with the sale
Run every fan through a fixed pre-sale funnel: collect data, then scripted escalating PPV
Segment/tag fans by spender tier and personalize instead of mass blasts
Cold outreach signs creators as a high-volume, consistent, tracked system
Productized paid-ad funnel makes creator signing predictable
Lead with intrigue/curiosity and push to a call rather than selling in DMs
Qualify creators against an ideal-client profile; disqualify red flags
Optimize acquisition on ROI/LTV, not cost-per-lead
Reels/short-form are the primary growth lever with hook-retention-payoff
Warm new accounts before adding any direct OF/adult link
Run a multi-platform/multi-account traffic mix; no single source carries it
Snapchat as an under-exploited OF traffic and monetization channel
Master/document each role yourself first, then delegate via SOPs
Delegate outcomes/ownership up an accountability ladder, not just tasks
Split the agency into departments under managers on a revenue-gated hiring ladder
Run ops off central databases/trackers so detail lives in systems, not the owner's head
Treat the vault/scripts as inventory with a weekly refresh-and-prune cadence
Same-day documented onboarding that also manages creator expectations
A defined niche/persona built on the creator's strengths separates earners
Volume and reduced record-to-publish friction beat production polish
Give every model a written character/persona to lift LTV via parasocial bond
Keep AI/real hybrid on OnlyFans because full-AI loses conversion on Fanvue
AI bot handles new-sub volume/24-7 chat; route spenders to humans
Learn and systemize each role personally before scaling/delegating
